posts.name: Joe Mendez
posts.cooked: I would like to try some California Chardonnay’s that don’t have a buttery taste. Any recommendations under $75 a bottle? I just ordered a bottle of Teeter Totter Chard to try but I want to try others.

posts.name: Andrea V a c c a r o
posts.cooked:
My two current favorites:

  • Chanin Bien Nacido Chardonnay (great value around $ 35-40)
  • Mount Eden Estate Chardonnay
posts.display_username: Andrea V a c c a r o

posts.name: Ch@rlie F|_|
posts.cooked: Arcadian Sleepy Hollow.

posts.name: Brian Tuite  GOML!
posts.cooked:
Rivers Marie - Joy Road, Bearwallow, B. Thieriot
Ancillary - Sangiacomo
Arnot Roberts - Trout Gulch
Ceritas - Peter Martin Ray, Heintz, Trout Gulch
Copain - Brosseau, Laurels Grade

user_status.description: Wine doesn’t have to be tertiary to be enjoyable

posts.cooked:
Crawford Family
Rivers Marie
Montelena

posts.name: Bud Carey
posts.cooked: Liquid Farm

posts.name: Michael S. Monie
posts.cooked: Two often recommended here: Stony Hill and Ramey’s single vineyards.

posts.name: Subu Ramachandran
posts.cooked:
Will make it easier for you. These are less than $50…

Au Bon Climat - Sanford and Benedict vineyard(You can get it for less than $30 and one of the best burgundian style chards out of California). Try the Bien Nacido if you prefer a little more richer style.

Sandhi - both Sanford and Benedict wine and Bentrock (Much in Roulot style, incredible minerality in a reductive style)

Mount Eden - Have had it only once and found it pretty good

posts.name: Ed Steinway
posts.cooked:
Forman. Ridge Estate Chardonnay has some oak but it is not buttery. And another vote for Mount Eden and Montelena.

Thanks,
Ed

posts.name: Humberto Dorta
posts.cooked: Rhys, kutch, unoaked alpha omega

posts.name: Mike Evans
posts.cooked: Morgan Metallico is usually a solid unoaked Chardonnay. Oak also isn’t an issue in Macon wines from Bongran, Roally, and Guillemot-Michel, or in Brun’s Beaujolais Blanc.

posts.name: Chris Trapp
posts.cooked: Rams gate makes some great chards!!

posts.name: Marc Hauser
posts.cooked: Hanzell Sebella.

posts.name: Brandon Redman
posts.cooked:
Bud Carey:

Liquid Farm

This. Start with the While Hill bottling as a great intro to the house style. It looks to be under $40 in quite a few places.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
Try the 2015s from Sandhi, the best they have made. The Sta Rita Hills bottling is 30 USD. Hard to beat that.

I’d also look at Pence and Varner, also in Santa Barbara and both sub-30 USD.

posts.name: Todd French
posts.cooked:
Stony Hill, Stony Hill, Stony Hill.

Smith Madrone is a really fascinating one, as they age it in oak but you’d never know it.

posts.name: Mark Gerdelmann
posts.cooked:
Fait Main. Just stunning chardonnay

Cheers,

mark

posts.name: J0hn J K@ne
posts.cooked:
Rhys
Liquid Farm
Shafer Red Shoulder Ranch
Ceritas

posts.name: Eric S n y d e r
posts.cooked:
William Kelley:

Try the 2015s from Sandhi, the best they have made. The Sta Rita Hills bottling is 30 USD. Hard to beat that.

I’d also look at Pence and Varner, also in Santa Barbara and both sub-30 USD.

Lots of good options in SBC, with many options sub-$40. I don’t drink a lot of Chardonnay, but when I do, this is my preferred area.
I haven’t tried the 2015, but also like Sandhi. Found at Whole Foods frequently.
posts.display_username: Eric S n y d e r
